properties.name: B6 boundary
properties.description: Scotland–England transmission boundary. The set of circuits whose aggregate thermal capacity sets how much Scottish generation can flow south at any given moment. Historically the single biggest binding constraint on UK power flows.
properties.notable: When north-of-B6 generation exceeds B6 capacity, the system operator pays Scottish wind farms to stop generating — the origin of the ~£1.5bn UK constraint payment bill in 2023.

properties.name: B7 boundary
properties.description: Second-tier north-of-England boundary, downstream of B6. Becomes binding when B6 headroom exists but B7 circuits saturate.

properties.name: North-of-B6 constrained zone
properties.description: All Scottish grid generation whose output must cross the B6 boundary to reach demand. Constraint payments in this zone averaged ~£2-4 million per day across 2023-2024.
